title: Methods and apparatuses for contention-based communication and associated communication system
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure provide methods and apparatuses for contention-based communication and an associated communication System. In a method of contention-based communication, a message is transmitted to a network node in a first contention frame at a first contention layer. The method further includes receiving first feedback information for the first contention frame from the network node indicating the number of frames which indicates an accumulative number of frames determined by the network node at the first contention frame as to be allocated to a second contention layer following the first contention layer; in response to a failure of the transmission of the message, determining, at least based on the first feedback information, positions of a second contention frame and an end frame at the second contention layer; and switching, based on the positions of the second contention frame and the end frame, to a sleep mode in a duration of frames at the second contention layer other than the second contention frame and the end frame.
claims:
Claims I/We Claim: 1. A method of contention-based communication, comprising: transmitting a message to a network node in a first contention frame at a first contention layer; receiving first feedback information for the first contention frame from the network node, the first feedback information indicating the number of frames which indicates an accumulative number of frames determined, by the network node at the first contention frame, to be allocated to a second contention layer following the first contention layer; in response to a failure of the transmission of the message, determining, at least based on the first feedback information, positions of a second contention frame and an end frame at the second contention layer; and switching, based on the positions of the second contention frame and the end frame, to a sleep mode in a duration of frames at the second contention layer other than the second contention frame and the end frame. 2. The method according to Claim 1, wherein the first feedback information further indicates a contention state, and wherein determining positions of a second contention frame and an end frame at the second contention layer comprises: in response to the first contention frame being an end frame at the first contention layer, determining the position of the end frame at the second contention layer based on a position of the first contention frame and the number of frames in the first feedback information; and determining the position of the second contention frame at the second contention layer based on the position of the end frame at the first contention layer and the contention state in the first feedback information. 3. The method according to Claim 1, further comprising: in response to the first contention frame being other than an end frame at the first contention layer, receiving second feedback information for the end frame at the first contention layer, the second feedback information indicating the number of frames which indicates an accumulative number of frames determined by the network node at the end frame of the first contention layer to be allocated to the second contention layer; and 

 wherein determining positions of a second contention frame and an end frame at the second contention layer comprises determining the positions of the second contention frame and the end frame at the second contention layer based on the first and second feedback information. 4. The method according to Claim 3, wherein the first feedback information further indicates a contention state and wherein determining the positions of the second contention frame and the end frame at the second contention layer based on the first and second feedback information comprises: determining the position of the second contention frame at the second contention layer based on a position of an end frame at the first contention layer, and the number of frames and the contention state in the feedback information; and determining the position of the end frame at the second contention layer based on the position of the end frame at the first contention layer and the second feedback information. 5. The method according to Claim 1, further comprising: retransmitting the message to the network node in the second contention frame; and receiving, from the network node, feedback information for the second contention frame and feedback information for the end frame. 6. The method according to Claim 1, wherein the first contention frame includes a plurality of timeslots, and transmitting a message to the network node in a first contention frame comprises: selecting at least one timeslot from the plurality of timeslots in the first contention frame for transmitting the message. 7. The method according to Claim 6, wherein the first feedback information further indicates a contention state, the contention state including a success state, a collision state, or a non-transmission state for each of the timeslots, the method further comprising: determining whether the message is transmitted successfully based on the contention state of the timeslot in which the message is transmitted. 8. The method according to any of Claims 1-7, wherein the message includes a request message, the method further comprising: 

 in response to a success of the transmission of the request message, determining, at least based on the first feedback information, a position of a data frame allocated for the request message at the second contention layer; and transmitting data to be transmitted in the data frame based on the determined position of the data frame. 9. The method according to Claim 8, further comprising: in response to the data frame being an end frame at the second contention layer, receiving feedback information for the data frame, the feedback information for the data frame including an accumulative number of frames determined by the network node at the data frame to be allocated to the second contention layer. 10. A method of contention-based communication, comprising: determining a contention state for a first contention frame at a first contention layer, at least one terminal device transmitting a message in the first contention frame; determining, based on the contention state, the number of frames for the first contention frame which indicates an accumulative number of frames determined at the first contention frame to be allocated to a second contention layer following the first contention layer; and transmitting feedback information for the first contention frame including the determined number of frames. 11. The method according to Claim 10, further comprising: in response to a failure of the transmission of the message by the terminal device in the first contention frame, allocating at least one contention frame to the second contention layer for retransmission of the message by the terminal device. 12. The method according to Claim 10, wherein the message includes a request message, the method further comprising: in response to a success of the transmission of the request message by the terminal device in the first contention frame, allocating at least one data frame to the second contention layer for transmission of data to be transmitted by the terminal device. The method according to Claim 12, further comprising: 

 receiving the data from the terminal device in the allocated data frame; determining feedback information for the data frame, the feedback information for the data frame including an accumulative number of frames determined at the data frame to be allocated to the second contention layer; and transmitting the feedback information for the data frame. 14. An apparatus for contention-based communication, comprising: a message transmitter configured to transmit a message to a network node in a first contention frame at a first contention layer; a feedback receiver configured to receive first feedback information for the first contention frame from the network node, the first feedback information indicating the number of frames which indicates an accumulative number of frames determined by the network node at the first contention frame to be allocated to a second contention layer following the first contention layer; a contention determining device configured to, in response to a failure of the transmission of the message, determine, at least based on the first feedback information, positions of a second contention frame and an end frame at the second contention layer; and a mode switcher configured to switch, based on the positions of the second contention frame and the end frame, to a sleep mode in a duration of frames at the second contention layer other than the second contention frame and the end frame. 15. The apparatus according to Claim 14, wherein the contention determining device is configured to, in response to the first contention frame being the end frame on the first contention layer, determine the position of the end frame at the second contention layer based on a position of the first contention frame and the number of frames in the first feedback information; and determine the position of the second contention frame at the second contention layer based on the position of the end frame at the first contention layer and the contention state in the first feedback information. 16. The apparatus according to Claim 14, wherein the feedback receiver is further configured to: in response to the first contention frame being other than an end frame at the first 

 contention layer, receive second feedback information for the end frame at the first contention layer, the second feedback information indicating the number of frames which indicates an accumulative number of frames determined by the network node at the end frame of the first contention layer to be allocated to the second contention layer; and wherein the contention determining device is configured to determine the positions of the second contention frame and the end frame at the second contention layer based on the first and second feedback information. 17. The apparatus according to Claim 16, wherein the first feedback information further indicates a contention state, and wherein the contention determining device is configured to: determine the position of the second contention frame at the second contention layer based on a position of an end frame at the first contention layer, and the number of frames and the contention state in the feedback information; and determine the position of the end frame at the second contention layer based on the position of the end frame at the first contention layer and the second feedback information. 18. The apparatus according to Claim 14, wherein the message transmitter is further configured to retransmit the message to the network node in the second contention frame; and wherein the feedback receiver is further configured to receive, from the network node, feedback information for the second contention frame and feedback information for the end frame. 19. The apparatus according to Claim 14, wherein the first contention frame includes a plurality of timeslots, wherein the message transmitter is configured to select at least one timeslot from the plurality of timeslots in the first contention frame for transmitting the message. 20. The apparatus according to Claim 19, wherein the first feedback information further indicates a contention state, the contention state including a success state, a collision state, or a non-transmission state for each of the timeslots, the apparatus further comprising: a transmission determining device configured to determine whether the message is transmitted successfully based on the contention state of the timeslot in which the message is transmitted. 21. The apparatus according to any of Claims 14-20, wherein the message includes a request message, the apparatus further comprising: a data frame determining device configured to, in response to a success of the transmission of the request message, determine, at least based on the first feedback information, a position of a data frame allocated for the request message at the second contention layer; and a data transmitter configured to transmit data to be transmitted in the data frame based on the determined position of the data frame. 22. The apparatus according to Claim 21, wherein the feedback receiver is further configured to: in response to the data frame being an end frame at the second contention layer, receive feedback information for the data frame, the feedback information for the data frame including an accumulative number of frames determined by the network node at the data frame to be allocated to the second contention layer. 23. An apparatus for contention-based communication, comprising: a contention state determining device configured to determine a contention state for a first contention frame at a first contention layer, at least one terminal device transmitting a message in the first contention frame; a frame number determining device configured to determine, based on the contention state, the number of frames for the first contention frame which indicates an accumulative number of frames determined at the first contention frame to be allocated to a second contention layer following the first contention layer; and a feedback transmitter configured to transmit feedback information for the first contention frame including the determined number of frames. 24. The apparatus according to Claim 23, further comprising: a frame allocator configured to in response to a failure of the transmission of the message by the terminal device in the first contention frame, allocate at least one contention frame to the second contention layer for retransmission of the message by the terminal device. 25. The apparatus according to Claim 23, wherein the message includes a request 

 message, the apparatus further comprising: a frame allocator configured to in response to a success of the transmission of the request message by the terminal device in the first contention frame, allocate at least one data frame to the second contention layer for transmission of data to be transmitted by the terminal device. 26. The apparatus according to Claim 25, further comprising: a data receiver configured to receive the data from the terminal device in the allocated data frame; a data feedback determining device configured to determine feedback information for the data frame, the feedback information for the data frame including an accumulative number of frames determined at the data frame to be allocated to the second contention layer; and a data feedback transmitter configured to transmit the feedback information for the data frame. 27. A communication system, comprising: at least one terminal device each comprising the apparatus according to any of Claims 14-22; and a network node comprising the apparatus according to any of Claims 23-26.
